vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permissions on /etc/shadow, a standard STIG hardening step. It does not modify services, bootloader, or kernel parameters, and the change is reversible. While incorrect permissions could affect authentication, the recommended mode follows typical secure defaults and can be applied safely.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential authentication lockout if permissions are set incorrectly; Requires root privileges
